pi_items.source: Confirmed as primary candidate URL in setup_azure_devops.ps1 (line 24), also validated via Azure DevOps Server initialization in environment setup
Confirmed in setup_azure_devops.ps1 (line 138) where project is created with this exact name, and verified in work_items.json seed data
Confirmed as valid Azure DevOps work item type per Microsoft Learn documentation on work item types and fields. Multiple Bug work items exist in seed data (work_items.json lines 57, 67, 77, 87)
Verified as highest priority level in Azure DevOps standard configuration (priority levels: 1, 2, 3, 4). Confirmed in work_items.json where multiple bugs have priority: 1 (lines 61, 71, 81)

Verified as valid state in Azure DevOps Agile process template for Bug work items. Per Microsoft Learn Agile Workflow documentation, Bug states are: New, Active, Resolved, Closed. Resolved state indicates 'solution implemented but not yet verified'
Matches verifier.py logic (line 72) which awards 25 points when critical_bug_subscription_found is true
Matches verifier.py logic (line 74) which awards 15 points when critical_bug_filters_correct is true
Matches verifier.py logic (line 83) which awards 25 points when resolved_subscription_found is true
Matches verifier.py logic (line 85) which awards 15 points when resolved_filters_correct is true
Matches verifier.py logic (line 94) which awards 20 points when assignment_default_disabled is true

privileged_info_summary: The TailwindTraders project is configured in Azure DevOps Server at http://localhost/DefaultCollection with synthetic work item seed data. The project contains multiple Priority 1 bugs and follows the Agile process template where Bug work items have states: New, Active, Resolved, and Closed. Priority filtering uses standard Azure DevOps priority levels (1=highest, 2, 3, 4=lowest). Notification subscriptions can filter by work item type 'Bug', priority '1', and state 'Resolved'. The task requires creating two team-level notification subscriptions with these filters and disabling a default team subscription. Scoring allocates 25 points each for subscription existence and 15 points each for correct filter configuration, plus 20 points for disabling the default subscription, totaling 100 points.
